channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Global shares are mostly higher
item.description: TOKYO — Global shares mostly advanced and Japan’s benchmark Nikkei 225 briefly topped 70,000 for the first time Tuesday before trimming early gains after the Bank of Japan raised its key interest rate to 1%. The quarter percentage point hike took the benchmark rate to its highest #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe Mail

item.description: Nine people have been killed and 25 others injured following a collision between a passenger bus and a National Railways of Zimbabwe (NRZ) train at a level crossing in Chiredzi. The accident occurred at around 0700hrs on Tuesday when an NRZ train travelling from Triangle [...] Th #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe Central Bank cuts policy rate to 30% as inflation remains contained and foreign currency inflows surge @ReserveBankZIM
item.description: HARARE – The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has eased its monetary policy stance for the first time this year, cutting the Bank Policy Rate by five percentage points to 30%, citing subdued inflation, exchange rate stability and robust foreign currency inflows that have strengthen #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Zimbabwe Moves to Regulate Cryptocurrencies in Major Digital Economy Shift
item.description: Zimbabwe Moves to Regulate Cryptocurrencies in Major Digital Economy Shift Zimbabwe has taken a significant step toward formalising the use of cryptocurrencies, introducing a regulatory framework that brings virtual assets under the country’s financial oversight system for the first time. The move is being seen as a turning point that could reshape how digital finance [...] The post Zimbabwe Moves to Regulate Cryptocurrencies in Major Digital Economy Shift appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: IDBZ gets substantive Chief Executive Officer
item.description: Nqobile Bhebhe, The Infrastructure and Development Bank of Zimbabwe (IDBZ) has appointed Mr Willing Zvirevo as its substantive Chief Executive Officer with effect from 1 June following a period in which he served in an acting capacity. Mr Zvirevo has been Acting Chief Executive Officer since 1 September 2025, after former CEO Mr Thomas Zondo Sakala stepped down in August last year upon completion of his 10-year tenure at the helm of the development finance institution. A seasoned development banker, Mr Sakala assumed the role of CEO in September 2015, succeeding Mr Charles Chikaura, and oversaw a decade of growth and transformation at the institution. Mr Zvirevo now takes over the leadership of the bank at a crucial stage as IDBZ accelerates the implementation of its strategic development and growth agenda, with a strong focus on infrastructure financing, resource mobilisation and sustainable development initiatives. Before his appointment as Acting CEO, Mr Zvirevo served as the bank’s Director for Resource Mobilisation and Climate Finance, where he played a central role in strengthening the institution’s capital base and expanding its funding partnerships. IDBZ Board chairman Dr Kupukile Mlambo said Mr Zvirevo had demonstrated exceptional leadership and expertise in driving key strategic initiatives for the bank. “The Board expresses its absolute confidence in Mr Zvirevo’s leadership, strategic depth and proven experience to guide the Bank towards fully achieving its developmental mandate and long-term strategic objectives.” Mr Zvirevo brings extensive local and international experience in banking, corporate finance and investment management to the role. Prior to joining IDBZ, he spent seven years in the Cayman Islands with the Citco Group, the world’s largest hedge fund administrator, where he gained specialised experience in alternative investment platforms, fund structures and complex financial products. He has also held senior positions at KPMG Corporate Finance, NMB Bank Limited, Sagit Finance House Limited and AMG Global Chartered Accountants (Zimbabwe), where he led the Corporate Finance practice. His appointment is expected to provide continuity in the execution of the bank’s strategic priorities while strengthening efforts to mobilise funding for infrastructure development and other national projects aligned with Zimbabwe’s economic transformation agenda. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.description: Police Issue Firearm Warning After Gun Incident at Eaglesvale and CBC Rugby Match Police have issued a fresh warning to firearm owners following an gun-related incident at a schools rugby match in Harare that is now under investigation. The Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) confirmed on Sunday that it is probing an alleged assault and firearm [...] The post Police Issue Firearm Warning After Gun Incident at Eaglesvale and CBC Rugby Match appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Armed Robbers in 1,282 ‘Inside Job’ Heists Across Zimbabwe, Police Commissioner Paul Nyathi Worried!
item.description: Zimbabwe finds itself grappling with a persistent and alarming wave of armed robberies, with police recording a staggering 1,282 incidents in the past year. This figure, while representing a slight decrease from the 1,340 cases reported in 2024, remains unacceptably high, prompting a stern call for collective action from law enforcement and the private sector. [...] The post Armed Robbers in 1,282 ‘Inside Job’ Heists Across Zimbabwe, Police Commissioner Paul Nyathi Worried! first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: Manyame warns against illegal land deals @zimsituation
item.description: Source: Manyame warns against illegal land deals -Newsday Zimbabwe THE Manyame Rural District Council (RDC) has issued a strong warning against the illegal occupation and sale of land in the Seke Communal Area and surrounding peri-urban farms, saying bogus co-operatives and land barons are defrauding unsuspecting home-seekers using forged documents. The warning comes amid a [...] The post Manyame warns against illegal land deals appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo

item.title: Pardon Mapondera: gone but not forgotten at Venice
item.description: The fifth and final piece in our series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Pardon Mapondera, the artist whose practice the room finally arranges itself around, treats discarded plastic and glass jars not as ecological signal but as ritual matter, and produces, in the process, the most quietly devotional installation in the Biennale this year. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Gideon Gomo: from stone to bronze, the ancestral algorithm at Venice
item.description: The fourth in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Gideon Gomo’s first sustained body of bronze work, paired with discarded vehicle number plates and Zimbabwean springstone, is the move of a sculptor at full maturity rethinking the material vocabulary of his country’s most internationally legible art form. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Shumba Maasai in Venice
item.description: At the 61st Biennale, the Zimbabwean artist’s day-long procession through the city’s campi, performed under the borrowed authority of a Murehwa chief, restages the social-sculpture lineage of Joseph Beuys for a contemporary African discursive practice. #Zimbabwe #Culture #ZimbabweCulture #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Franklyn Dzingai: family albums and the neural commons at Venice
item.description: The third in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Franklyn Dzingai’s reductive cardboard prints, built from the family album, the magazine clipping, the back-room studio photographs of mid-century Zimbabwe, turn out, on close looking, to be the most precisely engineered work in the room. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Felix Shumba: charcoal and the practice of disappearance at Venice
item.description: The second in a five-part series on Zimbabwe’s pavilion at the 61st Venice Biennale. Felix Shumba’s six-metre charcoal wall and his Telephone Reader series anchor a body of work that argues drawing is not the act of capturing the seen, but the act of staying present to what is on its way out of sight. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Eva Raath: a slow archive of stitched memory at Venice
item.description: In the first of five long-form pieces on the Pavilion of Zimbabwe at #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tendai Kuwanda

item.title: Isn’t it lovely: a week in Venice with the Pavilion of Zimbabwe
item.description: A letter from Venice, written in the wake of the opening of Zimbabwe’s #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Valentine Eluwasi
